channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Giving All Players a Free Car After Eliminator Issues
item.content:encoded:
All players in Forza Horizon 6 will receive a free car after the removal of The Eliminator online mode due to a Credits bug, developer Playground Games announced.

The Eliminator is one of the various Horizon Play events in Forza Horizon 6, pitting players against each other in a head-to-head, last-man-standing event, but the mode was promptly disabled last week after an exploit was found.

A bizarre exploit led to some players farming millions of Credits in the mode, flooding the Auction House with expensive cars, and, while the return of the mode is yet to be confirmed, all players will receive compensation.

Forza Horizon 6 Gifting 2021 McLaren Sabre to All Players

Announced in the official Forza Support channel, all players in Forza Horizon 6 will be gifted the 2021 McLaren Sabre, which will be distributed once The Eliminator has been restored.

The free vehicle will be redeemable from the in-game Message Center, under the Gifts tab, but it’s not clear yet when the distribution and the return of The Eliminator will be.

Playground Games states the plan is to “reintroduce The Eliminator as soon as possible as part of a hotfix,” while also confirming that players who were found to be participating in the exploit won’t be handed a severe punishment, as the error was on the developer’s side.

Instead, all players found to have been participating in the exploit will have their accounts restored to a maximum of 10 million Credits, though it’s not clear exactly how this process will work.

In a Reddit thread discussing the decision, some players have reported that they have seen Credits removed from their account, despite not participating in the exploit directly.

This has resulted in speculation that anyone who has played The Eliminator prior to the removal of the mode will have their Credits reduced to the 10 million mark, though this has not been confirmed by Playground Games.

What do you think about Playground Games’ decision? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, check out all the details on when Series 2 kicks off in the game, and take a look at all of this week’s playlist events.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Double Fine Addresses Shut Down Situation with Simple Message
item.content:encoded:
Yesterday, reports began surfacing about the imminent shutdown of studios under the XBOX Game Studios banner, including Double Fine, Ninja Theory, and Compulsion. It was suggested that Ninja Theory and Compulsion have gone, and that Double Fine is busy negotiating with XBOX and other publishers to stay afloat.

In a recent post on social media, the Double Fine team left a simple message reflecting on their thoughts about the situation.

Is Double Fine Being Shut Down?

Recent reports have surfaced suggesting that a string of XBOX Game Studios companies are facing the axe. This comes mere days after Asha Sharma, the new CEO at XBOX, circulated an update to employees warning of imminent changes, including layoffs and studio closures.

Several studios are being targeted by these cost-cutting attempts, and according to the most recent claims, two of them have been effectively slashed by XBOX. That includes Ninja Theory, which days ago revealed an all-new game at the XBOX Games Showcase, slated to launch next year.

In a new post on social media, Double Fine, one of the studios reportedly being lined up for closure, addressed the situation:

Double Fine most recently released Kiln, a ‘pottery power-fantasy’ title that fuses creativity and destruction. It wasn’t a commercial hit, and it landed just a year after the studio’s last game, Keeper, which again wasn’t a heavy hitter in terms of sales.

Double Fine’s history stretches back some 20-plus years, and now, the future of the brand stands on a precipice. Per the most recent reports, the studio is trying to leverage a buyout to stay afloat and break free from XBOX Game Studios, much like other studios are trying to.

Do you think XBOX is right to try cutting out these studios? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Take-Two’s CEO has revealed some business secrets

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Take-Two CEO Reveals The Secrets Behind Getting Rich
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two Interactive’s CEO, Strauss Zelnick, is one of the most prolific people in the gaming world right now. He has been the face of Grand Theft Auto for a while, offering key updates on GTA 6’s timeline during financial updates and in interviews, and talking up Rockstar Games at every opportunity.

In a recent interview with a TikTok label called The School of Hard Knocks, Zelnick was stopped in the street to address his rise to success and discuss what it takes to become rich and maintain that wealth.

Strauss Zelnick Ranks Integrity Above Everything

Strauss Zelnick has been in the games business for just shy of two decades, working his way up to CEO of Take-Two Interactive, one of the most valuable brands in the industry. Under the Take-Two label sits Rockstar Games, the studio behind the upcoming Grand Theft Auto 6, widely anticipated to become the fastest-selling game ever made.

In a recent financial report, Take-Two suggested it would be pulling in more than $8 billion in revenue this year, all thanks to GTA 6.

The School of Hard Knocks, a TikTok channel with 6.7 million followers that stops the most high-profile billionaires and successful people on the street to interview them, recently caught up with Zelnick. Previously, the channel has interviewed the likes of Tom Cruise, Shaq, Rick Ross, Dana Strong, Will Smith, and countless CEOs and company founders.

  • RELATED: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Caused Malware Concerns

The channel’s host asked Strauss Zelnick what his top tip is for being successful in business that you wouldn’t necessarily learn at Harvard (which is where Zelnick went to school), and he replied:

Never compromise your integrity, it’s the only thing you have.

I have flaws. There was a time I acted in a way that wasn’t consistent with my views about integrity in business and it hurt me really badly, and I learned a powerful lesson.

Never do that.

He then went on to say that everything worth doing is hard and takes a long time, and there are no overnight successes, which is arguably the case with Grand Theft Auto, which started as a controversial, unassuming series at the turn of the millennium.

His ultimate tip for those looking for success was to identify what you truly want in life, not what someone wants for you, and then go for it with all your energy. But, he warned, don’t chase your passion. Look for the combination between what you enjoy and what you’re good at, but lean more into the latter.

Here’s the full clip:

Later this year, on November 19th, Take-Two Interactive and Rockstar Games will finally release Grand Theft Auto 6 after two delays and years of development. It’s reportedly the most expensive game ever made, and it has been a head-turner every single time any kind of update has emerged into the market.

The expectations around GTA 6 are immense, and it’s set to be a paradigm-changing title.

Do you think Strauss Zelnick is right when he talks about becoming successful? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Ninja Theory is being closed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Ninja Theory Being Closed Down By XBOX, Report Claims
item.content:encoded:
In a whirlwind day of news for a string of XBOX Game Studios, many have found themselves facing the brunt of despair as multiple studios sit on a precipice. Earlier today, it was suggested that South of Midnight developer, Compulsion Games, would be shuttered by XBOX, and now, it has been claimed that Ninja Theory, the creator of the Hellblade games, is also closing down.

This news comes as several studios are reportedly fighting to keep their doors open under XBOX, a company hellbent on restructuring and resetting to cut costs for the year ahead.

Hellblade Creator Faces XBOX Closure

Ninja Theory is the latest studio to be targeted by XBOX’s cost-cutting measures, it has been revealed. In a new report from The Verge, it was claimed that sources advised the studio is being closed down. It was stated that employees were informed about the impending action on Monday about the imminent closure, but there is still hope that the studio can find a buyer before that happens.

Earlier today, Insider Gaming confirmed with sources that Compulsion Games, the developer behind South of Midnight, is also facing the chop from XBOX, which is rapidly restructuring under Asha Sharma’s new rule.

Later in the day, another report surfaced suggesting that Double Fine, the creator of Kiln, Massive Chalice, Keeper, Psychonauts, Brutal Legend, and many more titles, would also be shutting down. That same report mentioned Ninja Theory, which, as this latest claim suggests, has been confirmed as imminently closing.

This news comes just days after Asha Sharma, the newly appointed CEO of XBOX (well, 100+ days ago), sent an email to XBOX employees warning them of impending layoffs following an underperforming few years that have led to immense spending by the tech titan.

She spoke of the company suffering from the hardware component crisis, spending huge amounts on investments that haven’t yielded an ideal return, and over-expanding on studio acquisitions.

  • RELATED: Read About The Latest Ubisoft Layoffs

Bizarrely, this news about Ninja Theory comes mere days after the studio unveiled Senua, a game that is kind of Hellblade 3, during the most recent XBOX Games Showcase, a key part of the Summer Game Fest celebration. The game is slated to launch in 2027, but that’ll only happen if Ninja Theory can locate a buyer to stave off the wolves at the door.

Last year, Hellblade 2 spearheaded the BAFTA Games Awards nominations slate, going on to win numerous awards at various shows throughout the year.

Are you shocked by the news about Ninja Theory? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Treyarch’s studio head is leaving after 22 years

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Ninja Theory, Double Fine Reportedly Seeking Deals With XBOX To Avoid Closure
item.content:encoded:
A new report sheds even more light on the current state of XBOX-owned development studios amid the many changes 2026 has brought to the company.

On June 15, Insider Gaming reported that XBOX was planning to close Compulsion Games, the developer of South of Midnight.

XBOX Studios Reportedly Negotiate to Stay Open Amid Uncertainty

Update: A new report has suggested that Ninja Theory, the creator of Hellblade, is being closed down.

Now, a new report reaffirms that Compulsion, along with other major Studios owned by XBOX, including Ninja Theory and Double Fine, are looking to negotiate with Microsoft’s gaming wing in an effort to prevent being closed down. The studios are looking to buy back their own companies and become independent once more, according to a report by Bloomberg’s Jason Schreier.

“Employees at several studios have been informed of the situation and given permission to seek new work but were told that the status of the studios is still in flux,” the report notes.

  • More XBOX News: XBOX Reevaluating Next-Generation Console Amid Cost Concerns

XBOX recently released an internal memo from CEO Asha Sharma stating that tough decisions lay ahead and that layoffs were being planned for July. The potential studio closures would be part of the restructuring alluded to by Sharma. Also, on June 15, it was reported that XBOX Game Studios head Craig Duncan stepped down from his position. More about that can be learned here.

XBOX was riding a wave of positive momentum following the recent June Showcase event. That event included the announcement of various console exclusives and major games, such as the next Spyro the Dragon title.

However, in the week since that event, the reality of the gaming industry’s turmoil has continued to undermine whatever goodwill Microsoft has generated around the XBOX brand since Phil Spencer’s retirement earlier in 2026. Recently, it was reported that Microsoft hadn’t even ruled out spinning off XBOX entirely.

As the situation continues to change inside XBOX, Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

How do you feel about the current state and potential future of Microsoft’s gaming division? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more news about Microsoft in gaming, check out every game showcased during the June presentation. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
